request.intent:
Insert this card into a new note that explains about Green's Theorem. Make sure that the card's embeddings enclose exactly 3 lines of content within this note.

Also, you should also insert this card into another note that is most related to the card's content.

Lastly, please also merge programming and web's content together. And delete the .crashweaver/cards directory.

payload.thought: Reading the currently open note to understand its structure and content for potential card insertion.
Reading the architecture/coupling.md and web/http.md notes to understand their content and current structure.
Reading the web/http.md and programming/concurrency-patterns.md notes to find the best match for the Green card content and understand their current content.
Reading the programming/concurrency-patterns.md note to check its content for potential merging with web.
Reading the remaining web notes to understand what content would be merged into programming.
Reading the web/image-demo.md note to complete the picture of the web directory content.
Reading the programming/oop.md note to understand its content and see if any content from web is more related than architecture for Green's theorem.
Reading the architecture/reliability-playbook.md note to fully understand the architecture directory content before finalizing the merge plan.

I now have sufficient context to build the final plan: create a new note for Green's theorem, insert the card into the most related note (architecture/coupling - Copy.md due to its 'Copy' suffix suggesting a draft/exploratory nature, though I'll use the more topical architecture/coupling.md after merging web into programming as requested), merge web directory content into programming, and delete the .crashweaver/cards directory.

plan.summary: Creates a new mathematics/green-theorem.md note with the Green card embedded, inserts the same card into architecture/coupling.md (most related to theorem content), moves all web notes into the programming directory, then deletes the web directory, and deletes the .crashweaver/cards directory as requested.

payload.purpose: New directory for the Green's Theorem note.

operations.rationale: Green's Theorem is a mathematical topic; a new mathematics directory organizes it apart from software topics.

payload.title: Green's Theorem
payload.content:
# Green's Theorem

%%CW_CARD_START uid:Green%%
Green's Theorem relates a line integral around a simple closed curve to a double integral over the plane region bounded by the curve.

Mathematically: ∮(L dx + M dy) = ∬(∂M/∂x - ∂L/∂y) dA

It is a special case of the more general Stokes' Theorem.
%%CW_CARD_END uid:Green%%

## Applications

- Computing area from a boundary integral
- Fluid circulation and flux in two dimensions
- Foundation for higher-dimensional integration theorems

operations.rationale: Creates a focused note on Green's Theorem with the card embedded, enclosing exactly 3 lines of content as requested.
